evals.prompt: Apply a token foundation for this project. The taxonomy derives from a 'calm, precise, accessible' aesthetic direction with a minor-third spacing ratio and APCA-based contrast.
evals.expected_output: A working token foundation that applies the taxonomy — semantic color roles (primary, surface, on-surface, error, warning, success, info, disabled, overlay), typography tokens (font family, scale steps, weights), spacing tokens (named steps from the taxonomy), radius system, focus styles meeting WCAG 2.4.11, key status tokens (success/warning/error/info), responsive breakpoints, and core component tokens (button, input, card, modal). Each token is named for its semantic role and maps to a value from the taxonomy method. Accessibility claims are stated for foreground/background pairs. The output names the structure and roles — it does not reprint a values table or re-derive the taxonomy method.
assertions: Names semantic color roles (at minimum: primary, surface, on-surface, error, warning, success, info) — does not reprint a palette
Includes typography tokens: font family, scale steps by semantic level, weights
Includes spacing tokens named by semantic step, mapped from the taxonomy
Includes focus style token that references the recognized accessibility standard (WCAG 2.4.11)
Includes key status tokens: success, warning, error, info (each with background, foreground, border roles)
Includes responsive breakpoints with named steps
Includes core component tokens: button, input, card, modal at minimum
Does NOT reprint raw pixel/hex values as the deliverable — states structure and semantic roles
